title: Long if/else-if chains testing one value should use a 'switch'
messageFormat: Replace this chain of equality checks on the same value with a 'switch'.
summary: A chain of if/else if branches that each compare the same variable to a constant is a switch written the verbose way. The repeated variable name on every line hides the fact that one value is being dispatched, and it is easy to test the wrong variable in one of the branches.
A switch names the dispatched value once and lines the cases up so the full set of handled values is visible at a glance.
